title: Frontend Batch 39 - Pact Warning and Contract Test Stability Reinforcement
scope: Frontend test infrastructure and contract-test hygiene only
summary: Removed Pact V3 upgrade warnings, made Pact tests stable with neighboring helper tests, and reduced Vitest workers to 2 for repeated full-suite stability under WSL load.
testInfrastructureFindings: Pact V3 metadata caused repeated upgrade warnings.
Pact test module mocks were file-parallel-sensitive when neighboring tests imported shared API helpers.
Vitest maxWorkers=4 was still unstable under repeated WSL full-suite load; maxWorkers=2 passed.

validation.command: npx skills find pact contract testing vitest warnings
validation.notes: Results were general API contract testing skills; no new skill was installed.
Final runs: 1 file, 6 tests, no Pact warning.

validation.command: npm test -- --run <neighboring API/auth/helper tests plus pact>
validation.notes: Failed before env-based injection; final run passed with 10 files and 61 tests.
maxWorkers=4 failed with worker startup timeouts; final maxWorkers=2 run passed with 81 files and 559 tests.

remainingGaps: Full Vitest is stable but slow with maxWorkers=2.
Full Playwright e2e still needs Docker Desktop WSL integration or an already running local stack.
Pact provider verification was not run because this batch stayed frontend/test-infrastructure scoped.
recommendation: Proceed to Frontend Batch 40 - Full Vitest Runtime Partitioning Reinforcement.
